alerts.title: Houthi missile strike on Israel raises Red Sea shipping threat
alerts.summary: AP and Times of Israel report a Houthi missile attack on Israel that has stoked fears of renewed Ansar Allah strikes on Red Sea commercial shipping. The attack lands amid the emerging US-Iran MoU and despite proxy-deescalation language; Houthi status in global_metrics remains 'Elevated - Multiple Recent Attacks', with USS Eisenhower, Bataan, Carney, Mason and Laboon all on station in the southern Red Sea.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes continue in Gaza and southern Lebanon despite emerging MoU
alerts.summary: Five incident reports document fresh Israeli strikes: a Gaza City strike killing five police officers and a 13-year-old (Al Jazeera), a residential strike killing an infant and two family members (Al Jazeera, Times of Israel), and heavy bombardment 'reducing buildings to craters' in southern Lebanon (Guardian). The continued operations directly contradict the MoU's reported 'end of regional war on all fronts, including Lebanon' clause.

alerts.title: Russian milblog corroborates Oreshnik strike, identifies target as Belaya Tserkva
alerts.summary: Rybar (~178K views) now corroborates the ME Spectator Oreshnik report, identifying the target as Belaya Tserkva in Kyiv oblast and stating kinetic (non-explosive) submunitions were used — its third Oreshnik employment to date. The cross-channel confirmation upgrades confidence on Sunday's IRBM strike previously flagged as single-source, though Rybar concedes limited physical effect.

alerts.title: Republican hawks publicly attack Trump's emerging Iran MoU as 'nightmare for Israel'
alerts.summary: Middle East Eye and i24NEWS report Republican hawks are openly opposing Trump's emerging Iran agreement, framing it as 'not remotely America First' and a 'nightmare for Israel'. US sentiment is 54% negative with 'rubio' (17) and 'gabbard' (11) trending alongside 'trump' (76) and 'hormuz' (13), suggesting an intra-administration split is gaining media oxygen as the 30-day nuclear window approaches.

alerts.title: US deploys defensive 'umbrella' of tech assets to shield Hormuz shipping
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is deploying an 'umbrella' of defensive and technology assets to protect commercial shipping transiting the Strait of Hormuz, coinciding with the BBC survivor account of a prior Hormuz missile strike. USS Florida (SSGN) remains in the Persian Gulf and USS Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea, suggesting hedging against MoU collapse even as negotiations advance.

analysis_note: US-Iran MoU negotiations advance under a 30-day nuclear deadline, but live kinetic activity — Houthi missiles, Israeli strikes in Gaza and Lebanon, and a corroborated Russian Oreshnik strike on Kyiv oblast — continues to undermine the deescalation narrative as Republican hawks publicly oppose the deal.
